symptoms.symptom_name: Exertional dyspnoea that resolves with rest

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Effort-limited breathlessness that settles with rest fits deconditioning and restrictive chest-wall loading from morbid obesity.
PE dyspnoea is typically sudden and persistent at rest; exertional-only dyspnoea that resolves argues against it but does not exclude it in a high-risk patient.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bilateral grinding mechanical knee/hip pain, worse weight-bearing

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Bilateral, weight-bearing, grinding pain that is chronic and progressive is characteristic of advanced osteoarthritis.
Symmetrical bilateral mechanical pain without a single hot swollen joint is against septic arthritis.

symptoms.symptom_name: No chest pain, no rest dyspnoea, no haemoptysis, no calf swelling/pain

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of pleuritic chest pain, haemoptysis, rest dyspnoea and unilateral calf signs lowers PE probability but is not an exclusion given the VTE risk profile.
No chest pain, radiation or diaphoresis lowers ACS probability; objective testing still required to exclude.
Mechanical joint overload from class III obesity is the principal driver of the osteoarthritis.
Obesity is an independent VTE risk factor; combined with acute immobility it materially raises PE pre-test probability.
Absence of fever lowers the likelihood of septic arthritis, though it does not exclude it in isolation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Acanthosis nigricans on the posterior neck

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Acanthosis nigricans is a cutaneous marker of insulin resistance, consistent with the metabolic profile but not diagnostic of the acute presentation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Acute immobility / chair-bound with high fall risk

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Acute immobility is a major VTE risk factor that raises PE pre-test probability.
Inability to weight-bear reflects the severity of the mechanical joint failure.

symptoms.symptom_name: Loud snoring with daytime somnolence and fatigue

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Snoring and daytime somnolence in morbid obesity suggest OSA/obesity hypoventilation contributing to the exertional respiratory limitation.
Positional burning retrosternal discomfort in obesity is typical of GORD; relevant to management and to distinguishing from cardiac chest symptoms.
Borderline oxygenation supports significant respiratory limitation; a clinician measurement, not obtainable over chat without a home oximeter.

symptoms.symptom_name: Blood pressure 155/90, heart rate 95, respiratory rate 22 (clinician-measured)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Stable heart rate and mildly elevated BP with mild tachypnoea; reassuring against shock but requires in-person confirmation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Knee crepitus, reduced range of motion, medial joint-line tenderness (no effusion/erythema)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Crepitus, restricted movement and joint-line tenderness without effusion are hallmark examination findings of advanced osteoarthritis — obtainable only on in-person examination.

investigation_details.investigation_name: CT pulmonary angiogram (with D-dimer as a rule-out where pre-test probability allows)
investigation_details.expected_result: Not performed in this encounter; would objectively confirm or exclude pulmonary embolism.
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: A pulmonary embolism could be missed if it is not objectively excluded when clinical suspicion is not low.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective imaging is required to exclude PE where it cannot be confidently excluded clinically.

investigation_details.expected_result: Not performed in this encounter; would objectively assess for acute coronary syndrome.
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: An acute coronary syndrome could be missed if not objectively assessed when suspicion is not low.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: ECG and troponin are required to exclude ACS objectively.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: End-stage knee osteoarthritis on class III obesity
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Together these establish the leading mechanical/obesity diagnosis, but do not by themselves exclude the cardiorespiratory mimics.
Acute dyspnoea plus morbid obesity plus acute immobility is a VTE-risk cluster that mandates active screening, safety-netting and same-day in-person assessment.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Same-day in-person clinical assessment with objective observations (BP, HR, RR, SpO2, temperature)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The joints cannot be examined and vitals cannot be objectively measured over telehealth; a same-day face-to-face assessment is required to gauge severity and screen the cardiorespiratory mimics.
To objectively assess for acute coronary syndrome given exertional dyspnoea, hypertension and metabolic risk.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: D-dimer / CT pulmonary angiogram as clinically indicated
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To exclude pulmonary embolism given morbid obesity and acute immobility, if PE cannot be confidently excluded clinically.
To confirm the severity of osteoarthritis and inform orthopaedic planning — not urgent and not required before escalation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Immediate D-dimer as a stand-alone rule-out in a high-risk patient
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: In a high pre-test-probability patient a normal D-dimer does not exclude PE, so imaging (not D-dimer alone) is the appropriate rule-out; the AI Doctor should not rely on D-dimer to reassure.
